family_name: Prime labelings of zero-divisor graph families
strongest_honest_claim: The zero-divisor campaign now has paired verified p = 11 feeders on both active lines, a verified p = 13 feeder on the four-class Γ(Z_p × Z_25) line, checked Lean names for the abstract support-template reductions `zp_z25_support_template_reduction` and `zp_zp_z2_support_template_reduction_of_singleton_one`, and checked reusable family lemmas `singleton_one_lemma` and `three_interface_pack_lemma` on the Γ(Z_p × Z_p × Z_2) line. The strongest honest slice is that prime labelability of Γ(Z_p × Z_25) and Γ(Z_p × Z_p × Z_2) reduces to explicit classwise coprimality partitions on tiny support graphs, that the second family's actual support graph A-B, A-C, A-F, B-C, B-E, C-D collapses to the three parameter-sensitive interfaces A-B, A-F, and B-E after setting C = 1, and that the first p = 13 four-class stress test survives once the C block is refined to allow two doubled-prime exceptions into D. What remains is to formalize the actual support decompositions from ring structure and decide the six-class zero-slack feeder Γ(Z_13 × Z_13 × Z_2).
paper_title_hint: Support-template reductions and refined prime-block arithmetic in two zero-divisor graph families
theorem_slice_target: Checked reusable theorem-slice reductions for the zero-divisor campaign: `zp_z25_support_template_reduction` for the four-class family and `zp_zp_z2_support_template_reduction_of_singleton_one`, factored through `three_interface_pack_lemma`, for the six-class family after setting C = 1.
primary_theorem_slice_statement: For odd prime p, prime labelability of Γ(Z_p × Z_25) and Γ(Z_p × Z_p × Z_2) reduces to explicit classwise coprimality allocation problems on tiny support graphs; on the four-class family, the C block may include a bounded number of primes below half the interval provided their nontrivial multiples stay out of A ∪ B, and on the second family the actual support graph is A-B, A-C, A-F, B-C, B-E, C-D so that after setting the singleton class C to label 1 only A-B, A-F, and B-E remain parameter-sensitive.
fallback_theorem_slice_statement: If the paired theorem slice still does not close, preserve the smallest honest p = 13 obstruction statements about the arithmetic program itself: the old upper-half-prime-only F25 C subtemplate already fails even though Γ(Z_13 × Z_25) remains prime with a refined C block, and any failure at Γ(Z_13 × Z_13 × Z_2) should be recorded as failure of the current smooth-reservoir template rather than of graph primality itself.
parameter_sensitive_steps: Supply p - 1 C-labels for Γ(Z_p × Z_25) while allowing a bounded number of sub-half primes whose nontrivial multiples are pushed into D and still keeping A ∪ B clear of every C-prime factor.
Choose a four-label sparse barrier set for the B clique in Γ(Z_p × Z_25) whose nontrivial multiples spill into at most the 20 A-slots.
Count enough smooth labels for A ∪ E in Γ(Z_p × Z_p × Z_2) while keeping B as a disjoint large-prime block; the naive {2,3}-smooth template is now the first zero-slack p = 13 case to audit.
Keep the F block away from the prime support assigned to A in Γ(Z_p × Z_p × Z_2).
Show the leftover D block remains unconstrained once the required interfaces are satisfied.
fallback_target: If the arithmetic corollaries do not close, preserve the honest theorem-slice plus template-obstruction results: the old upper-half-prime-only C program on Γ(Z_p × Z_25) fails at p = 13, and any failure at Γ(Z_13 × Z_13 × Z_2) should be recorded as a failure of the current smooth-reservoir template rather than of graph primality itself.
next_blocker: The checked Lean reductions are still abstract support-template theorems rather than family-specific support decompositions from the ring law, and the six-class p = 13 feeder Γ(Z_13 × Z_13 × Z_2) still has to decide whether the current arithmetic story is a real theorem slice or only a promising scaffold.
next_feeder_why_decisive: With Γ(Z_13 × Z_25) now verified and the Lean surface upgraded through `three_interface_pack_lemma`, the remaining first p = 13 frontier is the six-class Γ(Z_p × Z_p × Z_2) line. Γ(Z_13 × Z_13 × Z_2) is decisive because the naive {2,3}-smooth reservoir is exactly zero-slack there, so success or failure will sharply determine how much paired p-range language the campaign can honestly use.
